decisions.summary: Fix = change `.gitattributes` to `.beads/issues.jsonl merge=union` (git BUILT-IN driver, needs no merge.<name>.driver config so it can never go inert). Verified empirically this session in isolated /tmp repos: divergent single-line appends on two branches merge clean via union (ort auto-merge), no conflict markers, all lines preserved; the same scenario with the default driver CONFLICTS.

Accuracy correction: the `merge=beads` line predates PAN-1841 (added in beads-init commit 7e3d6f68d8). PAN-1841's real fix is commit e236b02ffa = the code-level resolveMainPreferredSyncConflicts in src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts, scoped to the sync-main path ONLY. The issue's attribution is wrong but its technical claim (the attribute is inert) is correct. This explains why tick-12 still conflicted.

Single-JSON `.pan/*.vbrief.json` files (.pan/specs/, .pan/continues/) get NO content-merge driver. union corrupts single JSON objects; an ours/theirs driver cannot express 'main-preferred' because ours/theirs flip between `git merge origin/main` (ours=branch) and rebase (ours=main). Their conflict handling stays with resolveMainPreferredSyncConflicts (sync-main) + PAN-1124 immutability. Out of scope for PAN-1901.

The guard test asserts the TIGHT invariant (only `.beads/issues.jsonl` -> union; no *.vbrief.json / .pan/specs/ / .pan/continues/ path carries any content-merge driver), so it mechanically blocks BOTH regression classes: re-introducing an inert custom driver, and union-corrupting a single-JSON file. A doc comment alone is rejected per the 'enforce mechanically, do not rely on restraint' rule.

No `pan doctor` check and no `git config merge.*.driver` wiring is added. bd has no merge driver (`bd merge` does not exist; only merge-slot/duplicates), and any config-based driver reproduces the inert-fix bug (verified unset on the primary main repo). union is built-in and self-contained in the committed `.gitattributes`.

hazards.summary: union concatenates both sides' UNIQUE lines. If both sides edit the SAME issue's line differently, union keeps BOTH lines (two lines, same id, different content).
hazards.mitigation: This is expected and acceptable — `bd` reconciles duplicate IDs on load (operator-confirmed in the issue comment). Do NOT try to dedup in git or post-process the JSONL.

hazards.summary: Applying `merge=union` (or any content driver) to a single-JSON `.pan/*.vbrief.json` file corrupts it (interleaved hunks -> invalid JSON).
hazards.mitigation: Only attribute `.beads/issues.jsonl`. The guard test (W2 case 3) MUST fail if any *.vbrief.json / .pan/specs/ / .pan/continues/ path is given a merge driver. Do not broaden the attribute.

hazards.summary: The existing resolveMainPreferredSyncConflicts (merge-agent.ts) still runs in sync-main and treats `.beads`, `.pan/specs`, `.pan/continues` as main-preferred.
hazards.mitigation: union only REMOVES `.beads/issues.jsonl` from the conflict set there (strictly better); the resolver's 'all conflicts must be pipeline-owned' bail logic is unchanged. Do NOT remove or alter that resolver (NFR-2).

hazards.summary: Tests that run real `git merge` could accidentally operate on the live workspace repo.
hazards.mitigation: Create isolated temp git repos (mktemp / os.tmpdir), mirroring the scaffolding in src/lib/pan-dir/__tests__/auto-commit.test.ts. Never run `git merge` against the workspace. No fake-timer needed (git ops, not delays).

hazards.summary: This issue does NOT make `.pan` state fully safe; single-JSON files stay unprotected in non-sync-main merge paths (Flywheel raw merge, uat-assemble-deps.ts:48, verification-runner.ts:92) and the immutability it leans on is violated today (PAN-1728).
hazards.mitigation: Scope is the beads half only. Keep the residual gap explicit in NonGoals; do not silently imply the state-merge problem is solved. Related: PAN-1728, PAN-1650, PAN-1758, PAN-1770.

sessionHistory.note: Auto-planning complete: 2 beads (W1 .gitattributes union change, W2 guard tests). union fix verified empirically in /tmp. Single-JSON .pan files bounded out with technical justification.

pipeline.reviewNotes: No blocking findings; union gitattributes change and guard tests reviewed and focused test passed
pipeline.testNotes: Skipped: no code changed since pre-review verification gate
